title: Perioperative risk scores: prediction, pitfalls, and progress
chunks: Perioperative risk scores aim to risk-stratify patients to guide evaluation and management. Scores often do not generalize well to new data and require ongoing updates.

ASA score: 6 classes based on preoperative health. Median c-statistic 0.77 for perioperative mortality. POSSUM: 12 physiological and 6 operative variables, c-statistic 0.82. P-POSSUM improves calibration.
ACS NSQIP: 21 predictors from 1.4 million patients, c-statistic 0.83. SORT: 6 variables developed from >16,000 patients, c-statistic 0.91.
PreOpNet deep-learning using ECG data predicts 30-day mortality (c-stat 0.83) and MACE (c-stat 0.77). LLMs evaluated for perioperative risk showing moderate to strong performance.
Future: data integration (EHR+imaging+genetics), dynamic prediction with intraoperative time-series, continuous learning, clinical workflow integration.
Pitfalls: overfitting, bias from nonrepresentative samples, inadequate validation, small sample sizes. Models require external validation across demographic subgroups.
